title: Remove Warning Message for Variables Only Used as Delays
body:
> Thanks for taking the time to report this.

> What would you like added/supported?

```systemverilog
// File: dly_warning.sv

// verilator lint_off ASSIGNDLY

module dly_warning (
    input logic a_in,
    input logic [2:0] delaycw,

    output logic a_out
);

timeunit 1ns;
timeprecision 1ns;

time dly;

assign dly = 5 * delaycw;
assign #dly a_out = a_in;     // Warning ASSIGNDLY in this line has turned off

endmodule

```
In the above code, when I use `verilator --lint-only -Wall` to it, it shows the following message.
```
$ verilator --lint-only -Wall dly_warning.sv
%Warning-UNUSED: dly_warning.sv:13:6: Signal is not used: 'dly'
                                    : ... In instance dly_warning
   13 | time dly;
      |      ^~~
                 ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNUSED?v=4.202
                 ... Use "/* verilator lint_off UNUSED */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
$
```

However, `dly` is obviously used in the delayed assignment.

Although I know that Verilator is ignoring delays, I still want to use my files in other simulators. My suggestion is (1) to ignore UNUSED warning in this kind of situation or (2) make a new kind of warning message for this situation, so that people can turn off directly.

> Can you attach an example that runs on other simulators?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

Sorry, I don't have any other openly licensed linting software.

> May we assist you in trying to fix this yourself?

I hope so, but I don't have much time recently.
